meta.title: Type-Safe Validation with Zod
meta.excerpt: Building bulletproof form validation with single source of truth schemas that power both TypeScript types and runtime validation

props.body: Our forms handle sensitive customer data including contact details, addresses, and service requests. Validation is not optional - it is a critical security and UX requirement that runs on both client and server.

props.text: Traditional validation approaches create dangerous gaps between frontend and backend. Types drift, validation rules differ, and bugs slip through the cracks at the worst possible times.

idealFor: Single schema defines types AND validation
Same rules run on client and server
notIdealFor: Types defined separately in frontend and backend
Validation rules duplicated and prone to drift
No compile-time safety for validation logic

props.text: With Zod, we define schemas once and derive everything else. This eliminates an entire category of bugs where frontend and backend disagree about data shapes.

props.code:
import { z } from "zod"

// Define schema ONCE - types are automatically inferred
export const personalInfoSchema = z.object({
  firstName: z
    .string()
    .min(2, "First name must be at least 2 characters")
    .max(50, "First name must be less than 50 characters"),
  
  lastName: z
    .string()
    .min(2, "Last name must be at least 2 characters")
    .max(50, "Last name must be less than 50 characters"),
  
  email: z
    .string()
    .email("Please enter a valid email address"),
  
  phone: z
    .string()
    .min(10, "Phone number must be at least 10 digits")
    .max(15, "Phone number is too long"),
})

// TypeScript type is automatically derived
export type PersonalInfo = z.infer<typeof personalInfoSchema>
// Result: { firstName: string; lastName: string; email: string; phone: string }

props.text: On the client, Zod integrates seamlessly with React Hook Form through the resolver pattern. Validation runs on blur and submit, providing instant feedback to users.
The same schema runs on the server, ensuring malicious clients cannot bypass validation. Server schemas can include additional rules not needed on the client.

features.title: Schema Composition
features.description: Combine smaller schemas into larger ones. Our complete form schema is built from 4 step schemas.

features.title: Conditional Validation
features.description: Use .refine() for complex rules like 'emergency requests require phone number'.

features.title: Transform Pipelines
features.description: Use .transform() to normalize data - trim strings, format phones, convert dates.

features.title: Custom Error Messages
features.description: Every validation rule has a user-friendly message that appears in the UI.

props.text: Zod provides structured error objects that map directly to form fields. We transform these into user-friendly messages with consistent formatting.

notIdealFor: Validation that requires async database checks

props.body: Define schemas as the single source of truth. Let Zod infer your TypeScript types, power your form validation, and guard your server actions. The result is bulletproof type safety from form input to database - with zero type duplication.
